operating_state: Revenue increased 15% to $7.81 billion driven by COVID-19 antibodies, Trulicity growth, and strong performance from Verzenio and Jardiance. U.S. revenue surged while international revenue declined due to foreign exchange headwinds and generic competition for Alimta and Cymbalta. Volume growth of 20% was partially offset by price declines and foreign exchange impact.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved 110 basis points to 73.5% primarily due to favorable foreign exchange effects on international inventories, partially offset by COVID-19 antibody sales mix and lower realized prices. Research and development expenses decreased driven by lower COVID-19 antibody development costs, while marketing and administrative expenses remained flat.
material_events: Company repurchased $1.50 billion of shares under authorization program with $3.25 billion remaining and paid quarterly dividends of $885.5 million. Recognized acquired IPR&D primarily for Priority Review Voucher purchase and redeemed euro-denominated notes due 2022 at par value in March.
macro_exposures: Implementation of Tax Act provision requiring R&D expense capitalization will increase 2022 cash tax payments by up to $1.50 billion while favorably impacting effective tax rate by 4 percentage points this quarter. Foreign currency exposure created revenue headwind while COVID-19 pandemic continues creating supply chain disruptions and operational constraints.
forward_outlook: Management expects limited additional COVID-19 antibody revenue beginning in Q2 2022 due to variant efficacy concerns and competitive therapies. Effective tax rate projected at 13-14% for 2022 if R&D capitalization provision is repealed. Multiple Alimta generics expected to launch in Q2 2022 causing rapid revenue decline following U.S. patent exclusivity loss.
uncertainty_profile: Alimta faces imminent generic competition with pediatric exclusivity expiring May 2022 and alternative forms already launched. FDA issued complete response letter for Sintilimab requiring additional clinical study. Ongoing 340B program litigation and intensifying regulatory scrutiny around manufacturing practices could result in product recalls, fines, and approval delays.
